rows.finalText: For "What is the next right step in my calling", the clearest pattern sits in Purpose / Calling with a right path emphasis. Past opens with the Counterpart: the need to consider how the other party experiences this. This sets the emotional weather for everything that follows. Counterpart tends to draw your calling toward the other person's position, their motives, and the part of the story you do not control directly. Counterpart sets the right path ground here: whether this specific person is on a path that is genuinely right for them. The Mice in Present acts as the fulcrum, so this is the point where tone, timing, or choice can change the sequence. Mice colors your calling through the small repeated drain that weakens conviction or meaning if it is left unaddressed. Mice at the pivot names the right path pressure directly: small but persistent signals that something is being quietly compromised. The direction in Future comes through the Coffin: an outdated pattern ready to be released gracefully, once the middle card is handled directly. Coffin colors your calling through the part of the old path that has already finished, and the rest that is needed before anything genuinely new can take root. Coffin carries the right path direction: recognising that a path has ended and the next one has not yet appeared. The spread turns on the Mice more than any other card. The practical thread here is to patch leaks and simplify.
